rules_fired.because: Proteins lose activity rapidly above 4°C via thermal denaturation and proteolytic degradation. Even brief exposure to room temperature can reduce the activity of sensitive enzymes (reverse transcriptase, ligase, Klenow) by 20–50%. Keep vials on ice throughout the entire workflow. Vortexing creates a high-shear air–water interface that can unfold proteins; mix by gentle inversion or brief centrifugation instead.
rules_fired.cite: NEB Enzyme Storage and Handling Guidelines. Sambrook J & Russell DW (2001) Molecular Cloning 3rd ed., Appendix 1.

rules_fired.because: Proteins, oligonucleotides, and antibodies at low concentrations lose significant material to non-specific adsorption onto standard polypropylene and polystyrene surfaces within 30–60 minutes, particularly below 100 µg/mL. LoBind surfaces have reduced hydrophobic character; adding BSA or another carrier protein competes for adsorption sites and stabilises the stock at very low concentrations.
rules_fired.cite: Eppendorf LoBind Technology Technical Note, 2019. Kingston RE et al. (2003) Current Protocols in Mol. Bio.: nucleic acid quantification and dilution.
